hypotheses.cause: Config rollout introduced an invalid feature mode ('turbo_v2'), causing /work endpoint failures and wedging the queue consumer.
hypotheses.reasoning_summary: The app logs explicitly show 'work_failed' with reason 'invalid_feature_mode' and feature_mode 'turbo_v2' immediately after a process restart. Concurrently, the queue_depth remains stuck at 11 and jobs_processed is stuck at 1, indicating the consumer is wedged due to the bad configuration. This perfectly matches the 'Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers' runbook.

consulted_runbooks:
Queue backlog growing while consumers sit idle (score=0.0937): Symptoms: queue_depth grows monotonically; jobs_processed counter stops advancing; user-facing requests and health checks stay green — a silent backlog with no error logs.
Diagnosis: compare producer and consumer rates over a window; check the worker/consumer process state (paused, crashed, deadlocked); verify the queue itself accepts reads.
Remediation: restart (or unpause) the worker service so

Queue consumer scaled to zero replicas (score=0.0907): Symptoms: worker_shutdown (SIGTERM) in the logs and then silence; queue_depth climbing while jobs_processed stays flat; health checks green because nothing user-facing is failing yet.
Remediation: scale the consumer back to its baseline replica count. IMPORTANT: restarting the service is a NO-OP when zero replicas exist — there is no container to restart; an explicit scale-up is required. Treat th

Config rollout breaks requests AND wedges queue consumers (score=0.0767): Symptoms: work_failed errors naming an invalid setting (e.g. invalid_feature_mode) right after a rollout, while queue_depth sits above zero without draining and jobs_processed stops advancing.
Remediation: roll the configuration back AND restart the consumer as well — a consumer that loaded the bad config stays wedged after the rollback, so the backlog will not drain on its own. Verify both the er

proposal.hypothesis_cause: Config rollout introduced an invalid feature mode ('turbo_v2'), causing /work endpoint failures and wedging the queue consumer.

steps.expected_effect: Restores the canonical app configuration, removing the invalid 'turbo_v2' feature mode that is causing work failures.
Restarts the wedged queue consumer so it loads the corrected configuration and resumes draining the stuck queue backlog.

rollback_plan.expected_effect: Re-applies the invalid feature mode to restore the exact pre-remediation configuration state.
Restarts the worker to load the re-applied configuration, fully restoring the pre-remediation state.
